danger_signals.visual_form: Color-coded flash feedback on hit (green = high health, red = near death)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat assessment; red flash indicates enemy vulnerability and imminent defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Color-coded enemy archetypes (green tier = weakest, blue = mid, red = strongest)
danger_signals.meaning: Hierarchical threat level; red-tier enemies represent maximum danger in combat encounters

danger_signals.visual_form: Multiple NPCs attacking simultaneously in open level areas
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; frame slippage during multi-enemy combat indicates system strain and tactical danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Limited weapon pickups across levels; single-weapon carry limit
danger_signals.meaning: Resource danger; players forced into melee vulnerability, signaling hostile environment design

safety_signals.visual_form: Hyposprays scattered throughout levels or on corpses
safety_signals.meaning: Safe resource availability; indicates restoration opportunity in hostile space

safety_signals.visual_form: Cloaking devices located in level environments
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability signal; visual stealth capability provides tactical safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: Large unconfined areas; some levels comprise entire buildings
safety_signals.meaning: Environmental freedom signal; non-linear design allows escape routes and tactical repositioning

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Police force enemy archetypes; institutional military aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Government infrastructure; police station and institutional building environments

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Criminal organization combat units with distinct unarmed combat styles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Illicit criminal strongholds; underground and black-market facility environments

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: TCTF police forces serve as government enforcement arm
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Atmospheric Conversion Centers (air-treatment plants); totalitarian infrastructure; government facilities disguised as wilderness

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited habitable zones; regions disguised as wilderness preserves by government; visual representation of polluted Earth
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Population confined to government-controlled areas dependent on Atmospheric Conversion Centers for survival

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Omnipresent TCTF police forces; institutional architecture dominating levels; government surveillance implied through player opposition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: TCTF suppresses opposition; police actively hunt protagonist; institutional enemies prioritize government mandate over individual safety

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Syndicate strongholds and black-market facilities; dual enemy faction architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Syndicate operates as 'equally monolithic' counterforce to TCTF; competing for control; assassination and sabotage plot (Atmospheric Center malfunction)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Konoko transitions from TCTF operative (ally) to rogue agent (hunted); visual shift in NPC faction stance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: TCTF allies become enemies; behavioral shift from protection to pursuit; Konoko must fight both institutional and criminal forces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Atmospheric Conversion Centers as critical infrastructure targets; climactic level design emphasizing stakes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Syndicate escalates to sabotage; Konoko's mission shifts from faction survival to species survival; behavioral urgency in final confrontation

environmental_storytelling.example: Government facilities (TCTF stations, institutional buildings) vs. criminal strongholds (black-market locations, Syndicate bases) communicate political geography and power distribution through level layout
Limited ammunition and sparse hyposprays reflect post-apocalyptic resource depletion; scarcity reinforces survival setting and totalitarian rationing
TCTF guards in government infrastructure; Syndicate operatives in criminal facilities; spatial distribution reveals territorial control and factional geography
Atmospheric Conversion Centers positioned as climactic level; central to plot and survival; design emphasis communicates civilizational dependence and vulnerability
Large, fully explorable fourteen levels signal lack of confinement; open spaces communicate player agency against totalitarian control structures

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Non-hostile; provide mission support and information; institutional authority figures
Active pursuit; prioritize player elimination; behavioral shift from protective to aggressive institutional enforcement
Criminal combat operatives; distinct unarmed fighting styles by tier; actively sabotage and oppose government and protagonist
Progression from weakest to strongest; red-tier enemies employ advanced martial-arts combos; health flash feedback indicates combat intensity
Unlocks stronger moves and combos; behavioral progression reflects growing power and self-discovery; supernatural abilities enable survival against institutional and criminal forces

notes: Oni communicates world-state through dual-faction opposition (TCTF vs. Syndicate), environmental resource scarcity, and protagonist role inversion. Color-coding system extends from Marathon series, using tiers (green/blue/red) for both enemy classification and health feedback. Cyberpunk aesthetic (influenced by Ghost in the Shell, Akira) grounds visual communication in dystopian totalitarianism. Critical infrastructure (Atmospheric Conversion Centers) serves as narrative/environmental lynchpin, communicating species-level existential stakes. No explicit faction sigils or color identities documented in source; faction identity communicated through enemy archetype behavior, combat style variation, and territorial environment placement.
